cognitive.instructions:
Next you will find a series of statements on intercultural communicative competence (ICC) in its cognitive dimension.

There are no correct or incorrect answers. Please fill in the questionnaire, indicating how much you know about this facts.

Thank you very much for your cooperation and help.

Select the number corresponding to your answer:
1: Almost nothing
5: A lot

questions.1: I know the linguistic diversity of my own environment (dialects, indigenous languages and Creole, etc).
questions.2: I know some differences in the expression of emotions in other languages.
questions.3: I know what a culture is and I can give a definition.
questions.4: I know other languages in which aspects such as who can speak, who starts talking or when you can talk are different from mine.
questions.5: I know issues or behaviors that in other cultures are taboos.
questions.6: I know certain linguistic habits (greetings, saying goodbye, being courteous, etc.) that are different in other languages.
questions.7: I know the main characteristics of my culture.
questions.8: I know social practices or customs of my culture that are different from other cultures.
questions.9: I know some historical facts that have had influence on the evolution of other cultures.
questions.10: I know how language and culture influence the construction of people's identity.
questions.11: I know what cultures have influenced my culture.
questions.12: I know gestures that have different meanings in other cultures.
questions.13: I know routines or daily habits that are carried out differently with respect to mine.
questions.14: I know (in my own culture or in others) cultural practices that vary depending on social or generational groups.

critical.instructions:
Next you will find a series of statements on intercultural communicative competence (ICC) in its critical dimension. There are no correct or incorrect answers.
Please fill in the questionnaire indicating the frequency with which you make this statement.
Thank you very much for your cooperation and help.

Select the number corresponding to your answer
1: Almost never
5: Almost always.
critical.description:
Teachers: when I am teaching foreign languages ...

Students: when I am learning foreign languages ...

questions.1: We build knowledge from the diversity of languages and cultures.
questions.2: We reflect on situations, social behaviors or systems that promote power and submission relationships and prevent fair and equitable relationships
questions.3: We actively participate in the construction of knowledge to visualize social alternatives of improvement considering the different points of view, experiences and knowledge.
questions.4: We promote collective responsibility in achieving local goals with global impact.
questions.5: We enrich our perspectives to make decisions and resolve conflicts from dialogue with others.
questions.6: We promote dialogue and co-construction of knowledge around issues related to democracy, social justice, human rights, citizenship.
questions.7: We connect learning in the classroom with the service to the community to generate a positive impact both locally and globaly.
questions.8: We actively participate in community projects to promote positive changes in my community.
questions.9: We dialogue around traditional concepts such as culture, nationality, identity.
questions.10: We build critical reflections around the risks involved in generalizing, stereotyping and having prejudices against cultures.
questions.11: We involve the recognition of minority cultures and languages, visualizing the struggle of powers between global and ancestral languages as well as its relevance to the critical development of knowledge.

emotional.instructions:
Next you will find a series of statements on intercultural communicative competence (ICC) in its affective dimension.

There are no correct or incorrect answers. Please fill in the questionnaire indicating its degree of agreement on said statement.

Thank you very much for your cooperation and help.

Select the number corresponding to your answer:
1: Totaly disagree
5: Totaly agree

questions.1: I respect the values of people from other cultures.
questions.2: I get easily uneasy (I am worried, I am disturbed) when I interact with people from other cultures.
questions.3: When I am surrounded by people from different cultural origins I feel that my values are threatened.
questions.4: I always know what to say when I talk to people from other cultures.
questions.5: I am willing to distance myself from my cultural identity to better understand other cultures.
questions.6: I can be as sociable as I want when interacting with people from other cultures.
questions.7: I avoid those situations in which I will have to deal with colleagues with cultures different from mine.
questions.8: I respect the behaviour of people from different cultures.
questions.9: I would not accept the opinions of people from different cultures.
questions.10: I trust my ability to overcome the difficulties that arise in interaction with people from other cultures (uncertainty, anxiety, criticism, etc).
questions.11: I don't like being with people from other cultures.
questions.12: I usually feel little useful when I interact with people from other cultures.
questions.13: I am quite sure of myself when I interact with people from other cultures.
questions.14: I appreciate cultural diversity because it is a human condition.
questions.15: I usually get discouraged when I am with people from other cultures.

procedural.instructions:
Next you will find a series of statements on intercultural communicative competence (ICC) and the procedural dimension.
There are no correct or incorrect answers. Please fill in the questionnaire indicating the frequency with which you make this statement.
Thank you very much for your cooperation and help.

Select the number corresponding to your answer

1: Almost never
5: Almost always
procedural.description: When I am with people from other cultures ...

questions.1: I interpret their behavior from my cultural perspective.
questions.2: I qualify these people according to the values and beliefs of my culture.
questions.3: I remember my previous intercultural experiences that were successful to be more effective.
questions.4: I try to discover the objectives and purposes that other people have during the interaction.
questions.5: I previously analyze the situation and those aspects that can influence communication.
questions.6: I compare nonverbal communication (gestures, looks, distances, postures, etc.) of my interlocutor with the appropriateness of that situation in my culture.
questions.7: I adapt my interventions to the receiver if I think he/she does not understand me (I speak slower, I make more pauses, I pronounce in more detail, etc)
questions.8: I compare the way my interlocutor comunicates with the appropriate one to that situation in my culture.
questions.9: I analyze the customs and habits of the people of that culture from my own cultural perspective
questions.10: I ask the things I want to know to the people of that culture.
questions.11: I participate in other's people world's view even if that involves leaving mine aside.
questions.12: I share my opinions about cultural aspects with people from that culture.
questions.13: I reflect on what I already know about that culture when I find unknown information

start_screen: Welcome to the Critical Intercultural Resources Scale. This questionnaire will help you understand how you communicate with people from different cultures. Based on the results, you will be able to reflect on the best way to communicate appropriately and effectively.
